claim_boundary.faithful_lane: Thesis/Excel comparable lane. No risk tuning. Used for 1:1 Garrido continuity and baseline sanity.
claim_boundary.headroom_lane: Pre-frozen extension lane for learning headroom. Risk/PT knobs are justified as stress sensitivity and are not part of the 1:1 Garrido replication claim.

selection_rule.principle: Static/heuristic frontier first; no retained-reset outcome used to select the environment.
selection_rule.paper_priority: Prefer the full-horizon static frontier cell for the DQN memory paper, while retaining separate confirmatory sensitivity profiles for Excel-only and Pareto/resource wins.

shared_defaults.action_space: Discrete(18): six inventory levels x three shift levels

envb_frontier_v2.purpose: Primary DQN retained-transfer benchmark: full-horizon static frontier is off-saturation and not the S3/I1344 max-corner.
envb_frontier_v2.notes: Chosen after the full-horizon static grid completed. This supersedes the more aggressive phi=2/psi=1.5 cell as the primary DQN frontier benchmark.

envb_aggr_g24_raw.purpose: Confirmatory sensitivity for Partial Win A: exceed static policies on Garrido Excel ReT under aggressive stress.
envb_aggr_g24_raw.notes: This aggressive path is already launched in the Kaggle confirmatory kernel. The full-horizon static grid marks it as collapsed/off-saturation=false, so it is no longer the primary DQN frontier profile.

envb_cons_control_v2.purpose: Most promising route for Partial Win B: Excel non-inferiority plus resource/Pareto efficiency.
envb_cons_control_v2.notes: This is the conservative Pareto/resource path already launched in the Kaggle confirmatory kernel.

reward_gate: control_v1 vs control_v2 on training tapes
static-surface monotonicity and Spearman alignment with Excel ReT/service/resource metrics
